# AI Prompts

Prompt generation mode: master

Master mode gives you a more structured bootstrap prompt for planning, implementation, and verification.

Use these prompts as reusable starting points for common dashboard implementation tasks.

## Prompt Strategy

- `starter` and `master` are intentionally more generic so they stay broadly reusable.
- `pack` is the more complete setup: it adds richer best-practice prompts for specialized work.
- Use specialized prompts when you want stronger guidance around implementation quality, state handling, accessibility, testing, or review depth.

## Generated Prompt Files

- `bootstrap.md` — General project-aware prompt for any implementation task.
- `development-pipeline.md` — Multi-agent orchestration pipeline (HLD → LLD → TDD → Review per phase).
- `brd-from-mvp-status.md` — Generate a Business Requirements Document from an MVP status review file.
- `architecture-update.md` — Update architecture.md to reflect the current repository state via git inspection.
- `qa-pipeline.md` — Post-development QA & Test agent pipeline for gap analysis, test execution, technical debt audit, and QA report generation.
- `brd-from-qa-report.md` — Architect and BA agents review a QA report, then BA generates the next-version BRD to scope the next development phase.

## How To Use Them

- Start with `bootstrap.md` when you want one prompt that captures the project context and quality bar.
- Use `development-pipeline.md` when executing a full development phase with Architect, Senior Dev, and Context Engineering agents.
- Use `brd-from-mvp-status.md` after a status review to produce a structured BRD with P0/P1/P2 requirements and acceptance criteria.
- Use `architecture-update.md` after implementing one or more phases to keep architecture.md in sync with the codebase — run it before generating new HLDs/LLDs.
- Use `qa-pipeline.md` after completing development phases to run a read-only QA gate — it executes tests, audits gaps against BRD/HLD/LLD, catalogues technical debt, and produces a `QA-Report-{NN}.md`.
- Use `brd-from-qa-report.md` after a QA report is produced — Architect reviews technical feasibility while BA assesses business impact, then BA generates the next BRD (`BRD-MVP-{NEXT}.md`) to drive the next dev phase.
- Reach for a specialized prompt when the work is clearly about a table, chart, form, data integration, state handling, accessibility, testing, or implementation review.
- Edit the task wording in the prompt before pasting it into another tool so it matches the exact feature you are building.
